name: Homemade Egg Pasta (fettuccine)

ingredients.item: of 00 flour plus extra flour if needed
minutes. Remove bowl and flatten with hands.

method: Dump the flour in a pile on your countertop or cutting board. Make a hole in the center of the flour, enough for three eggs. Crack eggs into the hole and the scoop the flour towards the eggs and mix it with your hands. You want to control the eggs so that they don’t spread across your workspace.

Mix the flour into eggs until it starts to form a rough ball. Knead, turning the dough, knead, turn, etc. The perfect dough will be easily pliable and a little bit soft. If your dough seems too stiff you can add a little water to make it easier to knead. Form a ball, cover it with a small bowl, and let rest.

Set up the pasta machine and flour a large cutting board. After about 5 -10 minutes remove the dough ball and break ball in half, flattening one half with your hands. Flour both sides to avoid dough sticking to the machine, and run the flattened dough through the pasta machine to make it flatter. Start at 6-7 setting and go down to 3 and then down to 1-2. When the dough is flat enough, sprinkle a bit more flour and run it through your fettuccine setting. Cut to desired length with kitchen scissors and line up fettuccine on a floured flat surface. I have taken a picture of the final step and how the fettuccine should look when they are drying, there are pictures of the prior steps at the end of this recipe. Go through the same procedure with the other half of the dough. Let fettuccine dry. You can store fresh pasta in the freezer for later if you like. This will make enough fettuccine for 2-3 people. If you need bigger portions, you can add an extra egg and a cup of flour to the recipe for each person. If you have a thin pasta setting on your pasta machine you can also make angel hair pasta, but you would go down to 1 setting to make it thin. Serves 2-3. Olé

After flattening, run the dough through the fettuccine attachment. Carefully line up the fettuccine to dry on a floured surface, see the previous picture directly after the recipe for an example. Make sure you use lots of flour so the dough doesn't stick. It's very frustrating to be a novice at using a pasta machine and not realize how much flour is needed to prevent sticking. It's such a pain to clean the pasta machine so make sure to use enough flour. It doesn't make the pasta taste any different but it sure makes it easier to handle!
